evidence: Multiple heavily imported files are pure pass-through facades with "canonical" ownership claims but no behavior (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/runtimeError.ts`, `src/domains/generation/types/public.ts`, `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx`, `src/shared/hooks/mobile/index.ts`).

Runtime services expose implicit singleton/lazy-init access patterns rather than a single explicit lifecycle boundary (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports both initialize and lazy getter; `src/shared/components/ui/runtime/sonner.tsx` binds provider to `getToastManager()` and separately exports `toast`).

Cross-feature contracts around shot navigation are duplicated and partially divergent (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` defines local `SimplifiedShotOption`/`NavigableShot`; `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx` redefines shot shapes inline).

Project context bundles selection, CRUD operations, and user identity into one broad surface (`src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx`), while consumers/tests often need only a narrow slice (`src/features/gallery/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts` mocks only `selectedProjectId`).

Travel generation seam is split across two parallel APIs: `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideo/modelPhase.ts` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts` each define their own `buildBasicModeGenerationRequest`, `resolveModelPhaseSelection`, and phase validation logic.

`generateVideoService.ts` still owns large boundary-mapping responsibilities (image payload, pair config, request body assembly, and internal test surface), which duplicates logic already being extracted to `services/generateVideo/*` and increases drift risk at module boundaries.

Task-mapping seam returns ambiguous status in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts` by defaulting missing cache entries to `missing_generation`, conflating 'not loaded yet' with 'generation missing'.

Variant scope seam misclassifies an existing variant row with missing `generation_id` as `missing_variant` in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`, then `useLineageChain.ts` renders lineage errors based on that status.

Inline edit state is repeatedly remapped through large pass-through objects (state model + view model), including duplicate edit-mode adapter functions (`setEditMode` wrappers) rather than direct narrow interfaces (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:240`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:326`, `src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx:67`).

`useSegmentSlotModeData` memoization depends on full `props` and a freshly-created `state` object, so the heavy model recomputes on nearly every render, undermining memo intent while increasing closure complexity (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:287`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:420`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:472`).

Image lightbox render model assembles very large memoized object graphs with broad dependency lists and nested inline callbacks, making local edits high-risk for accidental stale deps or over-recompute (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:780`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:828`).
low_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Several hotspot modules rely on manual object reshaping and oversized memo/callback surfaces, increasing cognitive load and making internals harder to change safely.

evidence: Legacy structure-video compatibility fields are duplicated in two places: `StructureVideoLegacyUiFields` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/uiTypes.ts` and `LegacyStructureVideoFields` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts`.

Deprecation handling is inconsistent: `src/shared/hooks/useExternalApiKeys.ts` is a bare deprecated re-export with no owner/remove-by enforcement metadata, while other migration shims in this batch (`legacyStructureVideo.ts`, `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts`) use explicit governance policy objects.

Edge functions in the same subsystem use competing handler architectures: `bootstrapEdgeHandler` in `supabase/functions/get-task-output/index.ts` and `supabase/functions/get-completed-segments/index.ts` vs manual runtime/auth wiring in `supabase/functions/generate-upload-url/index.ts` and `supabase/functions/revoke-pat/index.ts`.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx` aggregates a very large composed prop contract (state, navigation, actions, shot management, optimistic state, modal state, task data, integration) and passes this through to `MediaLightbox`, indicating orchestration and view concerns are not bounded.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts` combines many concerns (variants, navigation, references, shot positioning, lineage, layout, button props) into one hook and one giant return object, increasing indirection and coupling.

`src/shared/hooks/useTimelineCore.ts` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts` both mix DB operations, cache writes, invalidation policy, and UI-level operation semantics in the same hook bodies.

findings.summary: Too many thin "canonical" facade modules blur true ownership boundaries.
evidence: `runtimeError.ts` only re-exports from `normalizeOnly`/`throwingOnly` (lines 3-10).
`public.ts` is only type re-exports while declared as canonical domain surface (lines 1-12).
`button.tsx`, `tooltip.tsx`, and `mobile/index.ts` are pass-through export files (entire files).
Each facade claims app/domain-level canonical role, creating many top-level hubs instead of stable, minimal public boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Define one explicit public entrypoint per subsystem (`errorHandling`, `generation/types`, `ui`, `mobile`) and remove extra wrapper chains; keep only facades that enforce policy or hide implementation churn, and migrate imports toward those few stable boundaries.

findings.summary: Global runtime services rely on implicit lazy-init/singleton access instead of explicit boot ownership.
evidence: `client.ts` exposes both `initializeSupabase()` and `getSupabaseClient()` that lazy-initializes if bootstrap was skipped (lines 12-19), permitting mixed lifecycle paths.
`sonner.tsx` injects `getToastManager()` into provider (line 29) while also exporting global `toast` (line 35), coupling consumers to shared mutable runtime state.
`useGalleryPageState.test.ts` mocks `@/shared/components/ui/runtime/sonner` directly (lines 61-63), showing feature-layer dependence on runtime singleton API.
findings.suggestion: Move Supabase and toast into explicit app-lifecycle modules: initialize once in bootstrap/providers, export read-only typed contexts/hooks to features, and deprecate direct lazy/global runtime access from leaf modules.

findings.summary: ProjectContext combines multiple responsibilities into one high-coupling ownership boundary.
evidence: `ProjectContextType` carries selection state, derived current project, full CRUD operations, loading/mutation flags, and user identity (lines 13-28).
Provider composes values from `selection` and `crud` plus identity into one object (lines 33-52), forcing one broad dependency surface.
Consumer test mocks only `selectedProjectId` (lines 7-9), indicating common narrow-use consumers still couple to the large umbrella context contract.
findings.suggestion: Split `ProjectContext` into focused contexts/hooks (`ProjectSelection`, `ProjectCrud`, `ProjectIdentity`) and keep a thin composition hook for places that truly need all three; migrate high-read consumers to narrow contexts first.

findings.summary: Shot domain contracts are duplicated across gallery modules with inconsistent shape ownership.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` defines local `SimplifiedShotOption` and `NavigableShot` interfaces (lines 10-20) and accepts both `allShots` and `simplifiedShotOptions` (lines 22-25), then performs dual lookups (lines 66-84).

`ShotActions.tsx` separately defines `simplifiedShotOptions: { id: string; name: string }[]` and callback shot shape `{ id: string; name: string }` (lines 14-21, 49-53), diverging from handler-local interfaces.
The same shot identity concept is modeled in multiple local contracts rather than a single domain-owned type surface, increasing translation and mismatch risk.
findings.suggestion: Create shared shot view-model contracts in one domain module (for selector options and navigable targets), pass a normalized `shotsById` map where needed, and remove local per-file interface redefinitions.

findings.summary: Travel task handoffs use overlapping payload contracts, forcing repeated fallback translation downstream
evidence: `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` emits many top-level fields plus orchestration metadata and conditional legacy-compatible fields (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:235`).

`buildIndividualTravelSegmentParams` merges composed family payload with another large top-level object and also nests `individual_segment_params` (`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:756`).

Reader code must probe many locations (`orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, segment payload, raw params) to reconstruct one semantic value (`src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts:45`, `src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx:148`).
findings.suggestion: Define a single canonical read/write contract for travel tasks (versioned typed DTO), generate both builder and reader accessors from that contract, and keep legacy compatibility at one adapter boundary instead of spread across payload builder, segment builder, and UI readers.

findings.summary: Join Clips page builds a large inline adapter bag that weakens boundary stability
evidence: The page passes `clipSettings`/`motionConfig` with many inline lambdas translating field names to `joinSettings.updateField(...)`, creating a broad ad-hoc boundary contract (`src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:394`).

Task creation for Join Clips uses a separate canonical snake_case contract and normalization path (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:197`), so UI and task seams are maintained independently rather than through one shared typed boundary.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared `JoinClipsSettingsViewModel` factory/hook that returns stable handlers and typed grouped settings once, and consume that same normalized model in both page/form orchestration and task request assembly mapping.

findings.summary: Inline edit internals mirror large state surfaces through pass-through mapping layers
evidence: `buildInlineEditStateModels` constructs very large mapped objects from hook outputs with many direct forwards (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:260`).
Equivalent edit-mode conversion wrappers appear in multiple places (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:240`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:326`).

`InlineEditView` then remaps the already-mapped state again into canvas/sidebar models (`src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx:67`, `src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx:177`).
findings.suggestion: Collapse these mirror layers into a smaller domain model with focused sub-hooks (`useInlineEditCanvasModel`, `useInlineEditSidebarModel`) that expose only directly consumed fields and shared adapter helpers (for edit-mode translation) in one place.

findings.summary: Segment slot model memoization is defeated by unstable object dependencies
evidence: `state` is recreated as a new object each render (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:472`).

`useSegmentSlotModeData` memo depends on full `props` and `state`, so it invalidates each render despite heavy construction (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:287`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:420`).

The returned model includes many inline callbacks and derived structures, amplifying recompute cost and closure complexity (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:330`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:391`).
findings.suggestion: Split `useSegmentSlotModeData` into smaller memoized selectors keyed by primitive dependencies, pass stable callback refs (`useEvent`/`useCallback`) instead of full object deps, and avoid passing umbrella `props`/`state` objects into model builders.

findings.summary: Travel generation boundary logic is duplicated across legacy and extracted modules, creating divergence risk in request-building behavior.
evidence: `modelPhase.ts` defines `buildBasicModeGenerationRequest`, `resolveModelPhaseSelection`, and `validatePhaseConfigConsistency`.
`generateVideoService.ts` re-defines equivalent logic (same model selection constants and validation behavior) instead of consuming the extracted seam.
The service file also exports a large `__internal` test surface, indicating helper-level testing is still coupled to the monolith instead of stable modular contracts.
findings.suggestion: Make `generateVideoService.ts` consume the extracted `generateVideo/*` modules as the only source of phase/pair/request mapping logic, then remove duplicate helpers and move tests to module-level contracts (`modelPhase`, `pairConfig`, `requestBody`) to prevent drift.

findings.summary: Generation-task cache synthesis reports `missing_generation` when mapping is simply not loaded, making the integration status dishonest.
evidence: In `enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData` (`generationTaskCache.ts`), `taskMappingStatus` defaults to `cachedMapping?.status ?? (taskId ? 'ok' : 'missing_generation')`.
When no mapping query has run yet, `cachedMapping` is undefined and `taskId` is null, so the row is labeled `missing_generation` even though repository lookup was never attempted.
Repository status vocabulary (`generationTaskRepository.ts`) has no explicit 'unresolved/not_fetched' state, forcing cache layer to guess and overstate failure.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an explicit unresolved status (for example `not_loaded`) in the mapping contract and propagate it through cache hydration/UI so missing data is not mistaken for deleted/missing generations.

findings.summary: Variant scope resolution maps a broken variant->generation relation to `missing_variant`, producing misleading lineage errors.
evidence: In `resolveVariantProjectScope` (`generationTaskRepository.ts`), `if (!data?.generation_id)` returns status `missing_variant`.
This path means a variant row exists but linkage is broken; semantically it is closer to `missing_generation`/invalid relation, not variant absence.
`useLineageChain.ts` converts that status into user-facing error text via `buildScopeValidationErrorMessage`, so the wrong status produces incorrect diagnostics ('Variant not found').
findings.suggestion: Return a distinct relation status (or reuse `missing_generation`) when variant exists without a valid generation link, and update lineage error mapping accordingly.

findings.summary: Phase config crosses boundaries through two different type entry points, weakening seam consistency for travel generation APIs.
evidence: `modelPhase.ts` imports `PhaseConfig` from `@/shared/types/phaseConfig`.
`generateVideoService.ts` imports `PhaseConfig` from `../../../settings` while also building `TravelBetweenImagesRequestPayload` from shared task types.
This creates two seam entry points for the same cross-layer contract and increases the chance of silent mismatch as either alias evolves.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one canonical `PhaseConfig` import surface for all travel generation request-building modules and enforce it with lint/import rules.

findings.summary: Legacy structure-video fields are duplicated across UI and migration type modules
evidence: `uiTypes.ts` defines `StructureVideoLegacyUiFields` (`motion_strength`, `structure_type`, `uni3c_*`).
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` defines the same legacy field set again in `LegacyStructureVideoFields`.
Canonical `types.ts` intentionally excludes these fields, so two parallel legacy contracts remain and can drift during migration cleanup.
findings.suggestion: Create a single exported legacy-field type (or delete one copy if no longer needed), import it in both modules, and keep legacy-field ownership in one migration boundary file to prevent schema drift.

findings.summary: Deprecated API-key shim lacks the sunset governance used by other migration adapters
evidence: `useExternalApiKeys.ts` is a deprecated compatibility re-export with no owner/remove-by metadata and no enforcement hook.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` and `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` both encode deprecation policy metadata and removal enforcement checks.
This inconsistency encourages indefinite retention of dead compatibility paths.
findings.suggestion: Apply the same deprecation-governance pattern to `useExternalApiKeys.ts` (owner, remove-by, enforcement/logging), or remove the shim after updating remaining imports to the canonical hook.

findings.summary: Frame-estimation defaults are inconsistent across adjacent tooling modules
evidence: `validation.ts` falls back to `24` fps (input mode) and `16` fps (backend default path).
`VideoSegmentEditor/constants.ts` defines `ASSUMED_FPS = 30` for frame estimates.
All are literals in local modules, so behavior changes depend on which code path computes frame count.
findings.suggestion: Centralize FPS defaults in a shared config module with named constants per mode (input-estimate, backend-default, editor-preview) and have both modules import those constants.

findings.summary: Deprecated compatibility hook keeps a second top-level API surface and obscures canonical ownership.
evidence: `useExternalApiKeys.ts` is only a deprecated re-export to `@/shared/services/externalApiKeys/hooks/useHuggingFaceToken`.
The test asserts identity between compatibility and canonical imports, which institutionalizes the duplicate entrypoint instead of driving callers to a single package boundary.
findings.suggestion: Finish migration to the service-owned import path, then remove the compatibility shim and repoint tests to verify canonical usage only (or remove shim-specific tests once shim is deleted).

findings.summary: `shared/hooks` mixes generic UI interaction hooks with vendor-specific backend orchestration, weakening directory role clarity.
evidence: `useHuggingFaceUpload.ts` handles auth lookup, Supabase storage uploads, and `huggingface-upload` function invocation, which are integration/workflow concerns.
In the same package, `useFileDragTracking.ts` is a reusable UI drag-state helper and thin DnD utility wrapper; these belong to a very different ownership boundary.
findings.suggestion: Move HuggingFace/Supabase upload orchestration to a feature or integration service package (for example under `services` or a `lora` domain) and keep `shared/hooks` limited to cross-domain UI/runtime primitives.

findings.summary: A domain hook test is housed in `shared/hooks/__tests__`, creating misleading ownership and change boundaries.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations.test.ts` imports from `@/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations` but is colocated with shared-hook tests.
The same test directory also contains true shared-hook tests (like `useFileDragTracking`), so the folder no longer maps cleanly to one layer's responsibilities.
findings.suggestion: Relocate `useGenerationMutations.test.ts` to the domain hook test directory (`src/domains/generation/hooks/__tests__`) and keep `shared/hooks/__tests__` exclusively for shared hooks.

findings.summary: A single `shared/lib/__tests__` bucket flattens distinct subdomain test ownership and makes package boundaries less legible.
evidence: Tests for media, utils, and debug modules are grouped together in one top-level lib test directory rather than near their subpackages.
Import styles vary by file (alias import for media vs relative import for utils/debug), reinforcing the absence of a consistent package-level placement rule.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a single test placement convention per subpackage (colocated `*.test.ts` or `__tests__` per subdirectory such as `media/__tests__`, `utils/__tests__`, `debug/__tests__`) and move these tests accordingly.

findings.summary: Edge endpoints implement request/auth/error flow through multiple incompatible patterns, creating structural duplication and inconsistent ownership.
evidence: `get-task-output`/`get-completed-segments`/`claim-next-task` use `bootstrapEdgeHandler` with shared bootstrap contract.
`generate-upload-url` manually creates client/logger and directly orchestrates method/body/auth/error handling.
`revoke-pat` uses a third shape (`initEdgeRuntime` + `authenticateRequest` + ad-hoc method/CORS handling), so equivalent endpoint responsibilities are spread across divergent templates.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all edge functions on one request pipeline (preferably `bootstrapEdgeHandler`), then extract reusable authorization guards (task ownership, project ownership, PAT checks) into shared modules so endpoints only contain business logic.

findings.summary: Lightbox orchestration is spread across oversized prop bags and state aggregators, obscuring component responsibilities.
evidence: `MediaGalleryLightbox` defines and merges many prop interfaces (state/navigation/actions/shot/task/integration) into a single component contract.
`useSharedLightboxState` composes many unrelated feature hooks and returns a broad object containing navigation, variants, lineage, references, shots, layout, and button wiring.
`ImageLightbox` then layers additional environment/shared/orchestrator hooks, signaling the boundary between container orchestration and presentational behavior is blurred.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a lightbox view-model/facade per mode (gallery, image, video) with narrow typed contracts, and split shared state into focused hooks grouped by lifecycle domain (navigation, variants, shot actions, references) instead of one aggregate state hub.

findings.summary: Timeline hooks combine persistence, cache invalidation policy, and UI action semantics in the same units, making behavior hard to reason about and evolve.
evidence: `useTimelineCore` contains derived selectors, queueing/persistence logic, mutation orchestration, and metadata-specific behavior in one ~678-line module.
`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` includes deletion business rules, Supabase queries/deletes, cache patching, invalidation, and UI callback assembly in one hook.
`useTimelineActionHandlers` accepts multiple pipeline bags and coordinates drop/duplicate/tap/endpoint/video-browser actions, indicating a central action-router rather than separated domain handlers.
findings.suggestion: Separate timeline domain services (DB operations + rules), cache synchronization adapters, and UI interaction hooks; keep hooks as composition boundaries over smaller services instead of embedding persistence and policy directly.

findings.summary: LoRA management conflates UI state, persistence policy, and domain actions, producing hard-to-predict state transitions.
evidence: `useLoraManager` owns selection rules, duplicate cleanup, persistence, trigger-word prompt mutation, save feedback UI state, and render helper output (`renderHeaderActions`) in a single hook.

`LoraSelectorModal` manages tab/edit/filter/pagination state and performs render-time synchronization using ref comparisons with `setState` inside render path, coupling prop changes to implicit UI transitions.
`useLoraSync` exists as another synchronization layer, indicating cross-module state ownership is fragmented and requires glue hooks.
findings.suggestion: Split LoRA domain state (selection + validation), persistence adapter, and UI presentation state into separate hooks; move render-time synchronization to effect-driven reducers with explicit transition actions.
